dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3552 IS: Haulers of Agriculture and Livestock Safety Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Fischer introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To amend the Motor Carrier Safety Improvement Act of 1999 to modify certain agricultural exemptions for hours of service requirements, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Haulers of Agriculture and Livestock Safety Act of 2025 or the HAULS Act of 2025.

section.header: Transportation of agricultural commodities and farm supplies
section.text: Section 229 of the Motor Carrier Safety Improvement Act of 1999 (49 U.S.C. 31136 note; Public Law 106–159) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking during planting and harvest periods, as determined by each State,; and
by striking subparagraph (A) and inserting the following:
drivers transporting agricultural commodities within a 150 air-mile radius from—

clause.text: the source of the agricultural commodities; or
the destination of the agricultural commodities;

paragraph.text: in subsection (e)(8), by striking during the planting and harvesting seasons within each State, as determined by the State, and livestock feed at any time of the year and inserting and livestock feed.

subsection.text: Section 229(e) of the Motor Carrier Safety Improvement Act of 1999 (49 U.S.C. 31136 note; Public Law 106–159) is amended by striking paragraph (7) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: The term agricultural commodity has the meaning given the term in section 395.2 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation).

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Transportation shall revise the definition of the term agricultural commodity in section 395.2 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations, to include—

paragraph.text: any nonprocessed product planted or harvested for food, feed, fuel, or fiber;

clause.text: livestock (as defined in section 602 of the Emergency Livestock Feed Assistance Act of 1988 (7 U.S.C. 1471)); and

subparagraph.text: the nonprocessed products of any nonhuman living animal, including—

paragraph.text: nonprocessed forestry, aquacultural, horticultural, and floricultural commodities;
fresh or minimally processed fruits and vegetables, including fruits and vegetables that are rinsed, cooled, cut, ripened, or otherwise minimally processed, as determined by the Secretary; and
animal feed, including the ingredients of animal feed.
